ticket.description:
Bonjour, nous regardons pour changer de transporteur. Pour le moment vous n'offrez pas la liaison avec ce transporteur qui se nomme Delivro. Voici le lien de la documentation de l'API : https://api.dispatchscience.com/reference/test. Est-il envisageable de votre part à ajouter le transporteur à l'extension de PluginHive? Merci et bonne journée!

------------------
Envoyé de : https://mcsl-w.pluginhive.io/home/views/orders/status/allorders

comments.body:
Hello Cedric,

Good day!

For shipping from Canada, apart from CanPar we support Canada Post, Purolator, FedEx, UPS, DHL Express carriers.

Unfortunately, Delviro carrier is not supported and we do not have any plans currently to integrate any new carriers.

Let us know for any further queries.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

Hi Team,
**Please share the below response with Cedric on the ticket:**

* * *
Hi Cedric,

Thank you for your patience and for sharing all the details.

We’re glad to confirm that we can take up the integration of the Delivro carrier and proceed with the development.

To move forward, we would like to align on the commercial aspect. If a pricing of **$200/month** works for you, we would be happy to prioritize this integration and proceed accordingly.

Once we have your confirmation, we will share a detailed timeline for development along with the expected go-live.

We truly appreciate your collaboration and look forward to working closely with you to make this integration successful.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

(Abhilash)

Hello Cedric,

I tried reaching you at +1 (819) 840-2757 but was unable to connect. In the meantime, I wanted to provide some clarification regarding the request.

The original requirement was to **integrate the Delivro carrier with our WooCommerce Shipping Services** solution.. This integration has been completed and has been live since last Friday.
The plugin is ready to display Delivro live rates at checkout and create shipping labels using Delivro services.

That said, we understand the importance of this workflow for your business and have already shared the requirement with our product team. They are currently evaluating the feasibility of supporting this use case and identifying the best possible solution.

In the meantime, if you would like to use Delivro with its live carrier rates, the integration is fully operational and ready to use.

We will keep you updated once we receive further information from our development team.

Once again, thank you for your understanding and patience.

Best regards,
Keerthi

**Call Summary:** Customer raised concerns about the shipping cost, we noticed the prices increase by a few dollars for Canada Post and Canpar.

********************************************
**L3: ** Case related to **Delivro integration - DELIVRO BOXES:**

I was testing the Delivro integration yesterday and noticed that Delivro supports label creation only for Delivro-defined boxes. However, the customer is currently using Weight-Based Packing method.

To use Delivro, they would need to switch to Box Packing and configure Delivro boxes. **The problem is that, this change impacts the rates for other carriers (Canada Post and Canpar)**. I tested this on the customer's store and noticed that even when using the **smallest Delivro box, the rates from the other carriers increase by approximately $1 to $1.50,** it **could be more** for other orders.

I have informed the customer about the need to switch to Box Packing for Delivro.

However, the person we are currently interacting with is the developer for the store and not the store owner. Once the store team reviews the setup and starts testing, they may raise concerns about the increased rates from the other carriers. At the moment, I do not see a workaround or solution for this scenario.

Hello Cedric,

Good day!

I am following up to let you know that the development of the feature to display fixed rates at checkout only for areas serviced by the carriers has been completed and is ready for release.

We are also considering the integration of GLS courier into our solution soon, and I will share further updates on this during our next call.

Once again, thank you for your understanding, and patience throughout this process.

Best regards,
Keerthi
Customer Success Manager | PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Cedric Beraud,

Touseef is currently unavailable, so I'll be assisting you.

The plugin automatically selects the cheapest available service from the enabled shipping options. In this case, the Canada Post Regular Parcel service cost is $9.53, while the Delivro service cost is $12.80. Since Canada Post Regular Parcel is the lower-cost option, the app selected that service.

If you prefer to use the Delivro service, you can manually change the selected service before generating the shipping label.

I have attached a short video explaining the same.

Please let us know if you need any further assistance.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Cedric,

Thank you for your reply.

The plugin prioritizes the shipping service selected by the customer during checkout. However, if the customer selects a shipping method that is not provided by the plugin or a Flat Rate or Free Shipping method, the plugin will automatically choose the cheapest available carrier service when generating the label.

As we currently do not have access to your WordPress admin access, we are unable to verify which shipping method was selected for this order by customer.

Could you please confirm which shipping service the customer selected? If possible, kindly share a screenshot of the shipping method shown on the WooCommerce order page for this order. This will help us investigate the behavior further.

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any assistance. We’ll be happy to help.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Cedric,

Thank you for providing the store access — we were able to investigate Order 244825.

Here is what we found: The shipping method the customer selected at checkout ('Deliveroo' at $12.99) looks like was configured directly in WooCommerce Settings, not through the WSS plugin. As of now, shipping method shown on checkout is also coming from WooCommerce Setting ('Livraison standard' at $12.99). Because the plugin only recognizes shipping methods that come from its own rate calculations, it treated the selected method as unknown and automatically defaulted to the cheapest available carrier — which is why Canada Post appeared in the backend.

To fix this going forward, the plugin needs to be the one showing the shipping options at checkout. You should remove the flat rate option from WooCommerce and use the plugin for shipping methods on checkout.

Once this is done, when a customer selects a carrier service at checkout, the plugin will recognize it and assign the correct carrier to the order automatically on import — no more defaulting to the cheapest option.

Please try this and let us know if you need any help configuring the carrier rates or automation rules within the plugin.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Cedric,

Our team has completed the investigation on order 250792 and we have identified the root cause.

The concern is that the shipping option your customers are selecting at checkout — labeled 'Deliveroo' at $12.99 — is a flat rate method configured directly in WooCommerce, not a rate coming from the PluginHive app. Because the app does not recognize flat rate methods as a specific carrier, it defaults to assigning the cheapest available carrier.

To fix this, you will need to enable the app's rates for Delivro so that the Delivro option appears directly from the PluginHive app at checkout. Here is what to do:

1. Go to the PluginHive app dashboard (App Settings > Carriers > Delivro) and confirm that the Delivro services you want to offer are enabled.
2. Go to WooCommerce > Settings > Shipping > your applicable shipping zone and confirm that the PluginHive (WSS) shipping method is active in that zone.
3. Place a new test order and this time select the Delivro rate that appears from the PluginHive app (not the WooCommerce flat rate 'Deliveroo' option).

Once the order is placed using the app's Delivro rate, the plugin will correctly recognize Delivro as the selected carrier and assign it accordingly.

Please make sure you have enabled "Real-time Rates" in WooCommerce.

Please try this and let us know if it works. If you run into any difficulty during the setup, we are happy to connect and walk you through it.

Hello Cedric,
 
Our team has completed the investigation on order 250792 and we have identified the root cause.
 
The concern is that the shipping option your customers are selecting at checkout — labeled 'Deliveroo' at $12.99 — is a flat rate method configured directly in WooCommerce, not a rate coming from the PluginHive app. Because the app does not recognize flat rate methods as a specific carrier, it defaults to assigning the cheapest available carrier.
 
To fix this, you will need to enable the app's rates for Delivro so that the Delivro option appears directly from the PluginHive app at checkout. Here is what to do:
 
1. Go to the PluginHive app dashboard (App Settings > Carriers > Delivro) and confirm that the Delivro services you want to offer are enabled.
2. Go to WooCommerce > Settings > Shipping > your applicable shipping zone and confirm that the PluginHive (WSS) shipping method is active in that zone.
3. Place a new test order and this time select the Delivro rate that appears from the PluginHive app (not the WooCommerce flat rate 'Deliveroo' option).
 
Once the order is placed using the app's Delivro rate, the plugin will correctly recognize Delivro as the selected carrier and assign it accordingly.
 
Please make sure you have enabled "Real-time Rates" in WooCommerce. 
 
Please try this and let us know if it works. If you run into any difficulty during the setup, we are happy to connect and walk you through it.
 


 
 

Thanks and regards,
Priyanka

comments.body:
Hello Cedric,

Thank you for your reply.

Touseef is actively working with the team on the Delivro label issue related to the route code. We'll get back to you with an update as soon as we have more information.

I'll also check the issue regarding Delivro labels being generated across multiple pages.

Regarding the Canada Post labels, under **Plugin > Settings > Carrier > Canada Post > Other Details**, the label size is currently set to **11 × 8.5**, which generates an A4-sized label with instructions. If you would like to print on **4 × 6 (A6 thermal) labels**, please update the label size to **4 × 6**.
Please note that this change will apply only to new shipments. For existing orders, please reprocess the shipment before generating any pending labels so the new label size is applied.

Rest assured, we'll get back to you with updates on the pending Delivro issues as soon as possible.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
